page: Google Cloud Platform n4a-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the n4a server family. It is configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture and 32.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a balanced resource distribution of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, which is typical for standard general-purpose cloud instances.

This server type does not include any local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance does not provide any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Because benchmark data is currently unavailable for this instance, performance characteristics are defined primarily by its dedicated CPU allocation and standard memory density.

The n4a-standard-8 is designed for workloads requiring a stable and balanced allocation of compute and memory resources. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, web serving, containerized microservices, and development environments. The lack of specialized hardware accelerators or local NVMe storage makes it a cost-efficient option for standard business applications that do not demand high-performance local disk I/O or GPU processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. With a balanced resource ratio of 4.0 GB of memory per core, this instance is designed for standard computing tasks. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring external network storage for data persistence. The instance also lacks complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Given its balanced resource profile and dedicated CPU allocation, the n4a-standard-8 is suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, application hosting, and development environments that require a steady balance of compute and memory without the need for specialized hardware. Its resource density provides a cost-efficient option for standard business applications.
